brief_document.title: Workflow detail — event history timeline
brief_document.purpose: Build the centre of gravity of the dashboard: a single workflow's full event history rendered as a readable, ordered timeline that tells the story of the execution at a glance.
brief_document.task: Build the workflow-detail feature: load a workflow's full event history via TanStack Query against the history API, project the aion-core Event list into a sequence-ordered timeline model with activity/timer/child lifecycles correlated by id, and render it as a vertical timeline with kind icons, envelope info, expandable payloads, child-workflow links, and loading/empty/error states.

requirements.title: History load and timeline projection
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL load a workflow's full event history via TanStack Query against the history API and project the events into a timeline model ordered strictly by event sequence number.
acceptance: src/features/workflow-detail/hooks/useWorkflowHistory.ts wraps the api client's getHistory in a TanStack Query keyed by workflow id + namespace.
src/features/workflow-detail/lib/timeline.ts projects Event[] into a timeline model ordered by the envelope sequence number.
The projection is a pure function unit-tested against a sample history (asserts ordering and one entry per logical event/group).
EventTimeline renders entries in sequence order.

requirements.title: Per-variant entries with lifecycle correlation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL render a timeline entry for each Event variant — lifecycle, activity, timer, signal, child workflow — correlating activity events by ActivityId, timer events by TimerId, and child-workflow events by child id into expandable groups, with activity retries shown as repeated failed attempts.
acceptance: Lifecycle events (WorkflowStarted, WorkflowCompleted, WorkflowFailed, WorkflowCancelled) render with distinct treatment; the closing event shows the terminal outcome and result/error.
ActivityScheduled/Started/Completed/Failed correlate by ActivityId into one ActivityGroup; repeated ActivityFailed attempts render as the activity's retry history.
TimerStarted/Fired/Cancelled correlate by TimerId; ChildWorkflowStarted/Completed/Failed correlate by child id.
SignalReceived renders the signal name and payload summary.
A test over a sample history asserts an activity with two failures and a success renders as one group with the retry history, and a child entry links to the child.

requirements.title: Envelope, payload view, and detail states
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL show each entry's envelope (sequence number, recorded timestamp), a kind icon and colour, a one-line summary, and an expandable payload view; and SHALL render explicit loading, empty, and error states for the detail view.
acceptance: Each entry shows its sequence number, a human-readable recorded timestamp, an EventIcon (kind icon + colour), and a one-line summary.
PayloadView renders the decoded payload (activity input/result, signal payload, error detail) collapsed by default and expandable on demand.
While loading, a skeleton timeline renders; when the history is empty, EmptyState renders 'this workflow has no events yet'; on error, ErrorState renders the cause plus retry.
A test asserts payload expand/collapse and that each of the three async states renders.

boundaries: SHALL NOT add live event streaming into the timeline — that is AU-009 (this brief provides the static history timeline and projection it extends).
SHALL NOT define the EventIcon/EmptyState/ErrorState/LoadingSkeleton components — AU-008 (this brief consumes them).
SHALL NOT define the namespace selector — AU-006 (this brief consumes the selected namespace in its query key).
verification: bun test passes for the timeline projection and detail view tests.
Timeline ordering is strictly by sequence number; no lint-disable comments added.

checklist.text: The detail view loads a single workflow's full event history via TanStack Query against the history API.
Events project into a timeline model ordered strictly by event sequence number.

Each Event variant renders a timeline entry: lifecycle (started/completed/failed/cancelled), activity (scheduled/started/completed/failed), timer (started/fired/cancelled), signal (received), child workflow (started/completed/failed).
Activity events are correlated by ActivityId into one expandable group (retries shown as repeated failed attempts); timer events correlate by TimerId; child-workflow events correlate by child id.
Each entry shows its envelope (sequence number, recorded timestamp), a kind icon and colour, a one-line summary, and an expandable payload view.
Child-workflow entries link to the child workflow's own detail view.
The detail view renders explicit loading (skeleton timeline), empty (no events yet), and error (cause + retry) states.

stories.text: As a debugging engineer, I want a single workflow's full event history rendered as an ordered timeline so that I can read the story of what happened — activities, timers, signals, child workflows, and the outcome — at a glance.

As a debugging engineer, I want an activity's scheduled/started/completed/failed events grouped and its retries visible so that I can see an activity's whole lifecycle as one unit rather than scattered rows.
As a debugging engineer, I want to expand an event's payload so that I can inspect activity inputs/results, signal payloads, and error details when I need them without drowning in JSON by default.
As a debugging engineer, I want to jump from a child-workflow entry to that child's own detail view so that I can trace a multi-workflow execution.

constraints.text: Match the apps/web stack exactly: React 19, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind v4, Bun, Biome (100-char), shadcn/Radix primitives, TanStack Query. No new framework, styling system, or state library.

Wire types come from the house generated-types pipeline (Rust #[derive(TS)] → src/types/generated/). The dashboard never hand-defines a type aion-core/aion-proto owns and never hand-edits a generated file.

The dashboard consumes the AW API/WebSocket contract; it defines no server endpoint, no WebSocket protocol, and no asset-hosting mechanism. Not-yet-pinned contract details are isolated in the api module for a one-file change.
Feature-folder convention from apps/web: each feature is components/ + hooks/ (+ context/lib as needed) + types.ts + index.ts. Barrels re-export; logic lives in named files.
Server state goes through TanStack Query; live updates flow through the WebSocket manager and patch query caches or local view state. No bespoke fetch-into-useState for server data.
Every async surface renders explicit loading, empty, and error states. No surface spins indefinitely or blanks on failure.

The WebSocket manager reconnects with bounded backoff on drop and the active view resyncs (re-fetch history/query, or replay after last-seen sequence where supported). A connection indicator is always present; a dropped socket never leaves a silently stale view.
The event timeline is ordered strictly by event sequence number and de-duplicates events arriving via both history fetch and live stream.
Biome lint + format clean (bun run check) and tsc --noEmit clean. No lint-disable comments to silence findings.
Namespace selection scopes every query and every subscription; switching namespace re-scopes both. No unscoped query when a namespace is selected.

resolved_context.intention: Build the human-facing operational web UI for aion-server: a browser application that lets an operator see which workflows are running and which have failed, drill into a single execution's full event history rendered as a timeline, and watch events stream live as they happen. This cluster builds the frontend only. It consumes the HTTP/gRPC list/query/history API and the WebSocket event stream that the server (cluster AW) exposes and hosts as static assets; it does not run the engine, persist anything, or define the API. It must match the existing house frontend stack (React 19 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind v4 + Biome + Bun, shadcn/Radix, TanStack Query, a singleton WebSocket manager with reconnect, generated types from Rust) exactly, so it reads as part of the same codebase as apps/web. When done, an operator pointed at a running aion-server can observe every workflow in every namespace, drill into any execution's full history, and watch events stream in real time — with production-grade loading, empty, error, and reconnection behaviour throughout.

provenance.quote: Build the human-facing operational web UI for aion-server: a browser application that lets an operator see which workflows are running and which have failed, drill into a single execution's full event
